entry.title: Washington Post: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Boris Johnson faces mounting resignations. How did he get here?
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia’s offensive gains pace in Donetsk; focus shifts to Slovyansk
  • Pope announces plans to expand women’s role in Vatican
  • Boris Johnson fights for political life amid resignations from his party
  • Wealthy Mideast nations boost spending to protect citizens from inflation
  • Amid energy crisis, E.U. says gas, nuclear can sometimes be ‘green’
  • I covered Somalia’s last famine a decade ago. It’s about to happen again.
  • #GentleMinions trend of suited-up teens irks — and amuses — moviegoers
  • NATO signs accession protocols for Finland and Sweden as Ukraine faces shelling in east
  • Far from Ukraine, Sri Lanka is the epicenter of a global crisis
  • Peter Brook, towering theater director, dies at 97
  • Chile writes a woke constitution. Are Chileans ready for it?
  • Boris Johnson’s latest scandal prompts resignation of top ministers
  • NATO takes major step toward welcoming Sweden, Finland to alliance
  • Miguel Etchecolatz, enforcer of Argentina’s ‘Dirty War,’ dies at 93
  • Twitter sues Indian government over content removal
  • Putin's aide warns US against pressing for war crimes court
  • Spain's famous Bull Run festival back after 2-year hiatus
  • Key witness: Netanyahu received gifts from billionaires
  • UN: 2.3 billion people severely or moderately hungry in 2021
  • Lebanon hopes to repatriate Syrian refugees within months
  • South African president decries deaths of 21 teens in tavern
  • UK's Johnson battles to stay in job after top ministers quit
  • Prosecutor: Fatal Italy avalanche likely unpredictable event
  • Hurricane Bonnie weakens, heads out into Pacific
  • Experts hope LGBTQ youth will call 988 — a new suicide lifeline number
  • Highland Park suspect’s father sponsored gun permit application, police say
  • Live updates: Highland Park shooting suspect to face more charges as community grieves
  • As a soldier, he escaped Nazi captors. At 97, he finally got his medals.
  • Threats from Highland Park suspect drew police attention in 2019
  • Trauma physicians have become depressingly prepared for mass shootings
  • There have been over 300 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • Post Politics Now: Biden heading to Ohio to tout work on bolstering retirement savings
  • Former White House counsel Cipollone to testify before Jan. 6 committee
  • Georgia grand jury subpoenas Sen. Graham, Giuliani and Trump legal team
  • Post Politics Now: Biden awards Medal of Honor to 4 Vietnam War soldiers
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Demand for workers remains strong despite mounting economic gloom
  • Hackers claim they breached police data on 1 billion Chinese residents
  • Oil prices fall sharply as recession worries grow
  • Dreading the knock brush before bed? Here’s how to make Slack less chaotic.
  • With an abundance of trees, Potomac Woods lives up to its name
  • Buyers failed the good faith test, and sellers contemplate next steps
  • What to look out for when signing a lease
  • The Redditors Should Know When They Are Conquered
  • Boston’s Michelle Wu Is a Mayor of Many Firsts
  • San Francisco’s School Decision Is Reason to Celebrate
  • Buy Now Pay Later Joins the List of Subprime Losers
  • How Britain’s Parliament Could Bring Down Boris Johnson
  • Why Payment for Order Flow Made Trades Free But Left SEC Skeptical
  • Inside Crypto’s Plan to Retool the Internet
  • What Are Stablecoins? Why Did TerraUSD Go So Wobbly?
  • Inflation is even worse than the official numbers suggest
  • Why the Buffalo supermarket slaughter should be a death penalty case
  • Give Ukraine more artillery, and let the Navy break Russia’s blockade
  • DeSantis is smarter than Trump. That may make him more of a threat.
  • The Post endorses Angela Alsobrooks for a second term as Prince George’s County Executive
  • The Georgia investigation remains Trump’s biggest problem yet
  • Why are we accepting the mass slaughter of animals through heatstroke?
  • Biden’s Ukraine strategy risks prolonging a violent stalemate
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 6 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-06 15:13PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wimbledon 2022 LIVE: Nick Kyrgios claims first set against Cristian Garin, Ajla Tomljanovic bundled out in quarter-finals

  • Cumulative fires threaten sensitive K’gari dune systems, report warns
  • Hundreds of health workers walk off the job
  • As it happened: Sydney flood risks grow as PM promises access to disaster payments from tomorrow; Nick Kyrgios accused of assault of ex-girlfriend
  • OzTag referee suffers brain bleed after alleged assault
  • Teacher shortage risks stunting students in maths and science, researchers warn
  • With just six women in lower house, Coalition will rue Ryan’s resignation
  • ‘Stunning blow’: Push to elevate role of women in Catholic Church fails
  • Dan Murphy’s enters race for tech talent
  • Andrew ‘Twiggy’ Forrest lifts stake in Zoe Foster Blake linked beauty business BWX
  • Big four banks pass on interest rate rise in full
  • The Wrap: ASX drops as commodity crunch hits

  • Warren Buffett-backed Chinese company topples Tesla
  • NRL-themed hotel on the way as deals hit $2b
  • To advance, Australia must think of the children – not their parents
  • Melbourne theatre’s ‘digital pivot’: pandemic thing or a revolution?
  • Around the World in 80 Days gets more worldly
  • ‘No, that’ll never work’: Why Courtney Act knocked back the idea for her TV pilot

  • Tom Hanks reveals his thoughts on Tim Allen not voicing Lightyear
  • Thor’s new self-mocking spectacle wants to be both silly and serious

  • Floodwaters still high as rain moves on

  • An orange flame will be the signal you’re cooking with (renewable) gas

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Guardian: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Joe Biden heads to Ohio to speak to talk up economic policy as poll ratings sag – live
  • Boris Johnson grilled by senior MPs as more ministers resign and Michael Gove joins calls for PM to step down – live
  • Wimbledon quarter-finals: Fritz v Nadal, Garín v Kyrgios; Halep and Rybakina into semis – live!

  • Juventus agree deal to make Di María second free signing with Pogba
  • ‘Let’s leave the city! Let’s get a dog! Let’s get a divorce!’ Do we regret our pandemic life changes?
  • Global dismay as supreme court ruling leaves Biden’s climate policy in tatters
  • This mutiny should be the end of Johnson. But never underestimate his sheer lust for power | Martin Kettle
  • ‘First modern novel – oldest language’: Sanskrit translation of Don Quixote rescued from oblivion
  • Smuggling operation sent thousands across Channel illegally in small boats
  • Conjuring Kesha: the pop star’s ghost-hunting show is a bizarre blast
  • A moment that changed me: I fled the Gaza blockade to study – but my freedom came at a cost
  • Five stand trial for sedition in Hong Kong over children’s books about sheep

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-06 14:55PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Beijing’s top diplomat in Hong Kong urges foreign consulates to present city in ‘more objective manner’ at briefing
  • Owners of Hong Kong sawmill appeal for help to move operations after being told to make way for public housing
  • New Hong Kong leader John Lee to set up 4 task forces on key livelihood issues, tells ministers to come up with policy targets by end of August
  • Hong Kong Book Fair organiser dismisses suggestions of censorship despite some publishers being banned from event
  • It’s time to accept Basic Law, former Hong Kong leader CY Leung tells opposition camp, supporters
  • Coronavirus: charges dropped against 3 chiefs of Hong Kong Scout Association accused of hosting banquet that violated social-distancing curbs
  • Tianqi Lithium to offer Hong Kong IPO at top of price range, as improving sentiment offers hope for companies awaiting the ‘right moment’
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong will not return to toughest control rules for arrivals, John Lee says, while also expressing hope to meet Beijing ‘halfway’ on border reopening; 2,815 cases logged
  • Hong Kong sectors rush to hold seminars to study Xi Jinping’s speech, sparking debate on effectiveness of such ‘mainland-style’ forums
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee in Legco: city not ‘lying flat’ in pandemic fight, PCR tests can be extended to exempt residents from restrictions
  • New Chief Executive, John Lee, must streamline planning process, boost transitional homes to address Hong Kong’s housing crisis, experts say
  • Hong Kong justice secretary and lawyer groups condemn intimidation of judiciary staff after parcel with white powder sent to High Court
  • 93 Hong Kong students attain perfect score in International Baccalaureate exams, 37 fewer than last year
  • Hong Kong’s premium office vacancy rate hits 19-year high of 12 per cent as more companies adopt work-from-home policies to counter downturn
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong must rein in spread of Omicron subvariants, boost quarantine capacity to help reunite cross-border families, analysts say
  • Hong Kong is now better equipped to deal with Covid-19 and open up to the world
  • China Evergrande launches first EV, the Hengchi 5, at just over half the price of Tesla’s Model Y as embattled developer aims to revive fortunes
  • China-US ties: foreign ministers to canvas friction points on G20 sidelines but no breakthrough expected
  • China ready for ‘golden era’ with the Philippines under Marcos Jnr
  • Chinese astronomy satellite discovers strongest magnetic field found in the universe so far
  • Beijing brings in vaccine mandate as city reports cases of new Omicron subvariant
  • China’s all-purpose WeChat app lets users register two accounts with one phone number in pilot test
  • Chinese pork stocks including WH Group recede as nation’s top planning body cracks down on misconduct, ‘vile speculation’ stoking hog prices
  • Alibaba’s home city removes name of Chinese e-commerce giant from future metro map
  • Tencent to close Steam-like mobile game portal as market prospects dim under tight regulation
  • Russian warships sailing near Taiwan puts Beijing in ‘an awkward position’, say analysts
  • China delivers US$37 million in aid to Afghanistan, fulfilling promise to Taliban
  • US pushes Netherlands to ban Dutch tech supplier ASML from selling older semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China
  • Belarus’s JD.com and Douyin online stores woo Chinese consumers with chocolates and crisps
  • Chinese health officials suspended after refusing to investigate forced removal of couple’s baby
  • ‘Not a joint drill’: Chinese frigate ‘sent to monitor Russian warship’ near Diaoyus
  • Pope hopes deal with China on bishop appointments will be renewed soon
  • Huawei founder Ren Zhengfei seeks employee input on business direction amid struggle to reinvent company under US sanctions
  • US receive over 400 requests to keep China tariffs, Joe Biden still ‘figuring it out’
  • Calling Capstone: Nasa hopeful it can resume contact with spacecraft heading for moon
  • How Nato’s stance on China has changed, and why it matters
  • Chinese scientists try to reduce space junk by using drag sail on rocket
  • China inflation: Beijing ready to tap pork reserves as global price rises hit closer to home
  • Chinese data centre operator GDS sees Singapore as hub for its Southeast Asia expansion plans
  • Top US and Chinese diplomats to meet on sidelines of G20 this week
  • US-China trade: what a tariff removal would mean for Hong Kong firms that have invested overseas
  • Coronavirus: New cases top 45,000 in Japan, nearly double last week’s
  • Australia will avoid engaging Asean through China ‘prism’, Foreign Minister Penny Wong says
  • Malaysian national Kalwant Singh among two men to be hung at dawn in Singapore after appeal is dismissed
  • Global food fears ease as price of cooking oil and grains plummets
  • ‘Ferdinand Marcos Airport’ proposal by Philippine President Marcos Jnr’s ally draws sharp criticism
  • Japanese grandpa turns accidental YouTube star with ‘warm and peaceful’ art tutorials
  • Indonesia’s first foot-and-mouth outbreak in 36 years threatens to upend Eid ul-Adha celebrations
  • Japan scientists clone freeze-dried mice in bid to beat extinction
  • South China Sea: Philippines’ Marcos wants ‘to resolve the conflicts’ with Beijing, says he’s open to working with China’s military
  • Will an Upper House election win allow the LDP to amend Japan’s pacifist constitution?
  • Muslim musicians in Kashmir test India’s limits on free speech through ‘conscious music’
  • UK PM Boris Johnson vows to ‘keep going’, says he’ll stay in job despite further resignations from his own party
  • Britain’s new finance minister Nadhim Zahawi inherits an economic crisis
  • ‘No safe place’ from artillery as Russians advance in Ukraine’s Donetsk
  • OPEC’S Secretary General Mohammad Barkindo dies at 63
  • UK newspapers: ‘Game over’ for Boris Johnson after key ministers quit
  • Parents of boy, 2, found alone at US July 4 parade shooting among dead
  • CERN scientists observe 3 ‘exotic’ new particles as Large Hadron Collider comes back online
  • Rudy Giuliani’s testimony sought in Donald Trump election probe
  • UK’s Boris Johnson on the brink as top ministers quit
  • July 4 shooter planned attack for weeks, wore women’s clothing to escape, police say
  • UK PM Boris Johnson ‘forgot’ about groping complaints against MP
  • Tesla in for further setback after losing EV crown to BYD, as some production is suspended to upgrade Shanghai assembly line
  • Chinese stocks to continue outgunning global peers as UBS wealth managers join bullish camp on second-half outlook
  • China Huadian’s renewable energy unit files for US$4.5 billion Shanghai IPO to fund expansion of solar and wind capacity
  • China is in no danger of losing its supply chain supremacy to India or Vietnam
  • Chinese developer Fantasia gets reprieve from creditors on US$108 million bond repayment
  • Hong Kong stocks weaken as mass Covid-19 testing reignites China lockdown fears while Alibaba gains on price upgrades
  • Shanghai’s home rental market suffers as owners struggle to replace tenants who fled town and displaced workers during lockdown crisis
  • How lonely is China’s Gen Z? Tencent-backed social networking app Soul poses the question as it files for Hong Kong IPO
  • What a strong dollar means for foreign investors in China
  • How geopolitics and Fed policies are putting US external sustainability at risk
  • China’s BYD takes Tesla’s crown as the world’s biggest electric vehicle producer by sales
  • World’s 50 Best Restaurants 2022 51-100 list: Hong Kong, Singapore each with 2 new entries; 7 Asian cities make up 14 positions
  • How to avoid childhood obesity in your family: experts say to make sure your kids eat healthy food, exercise and get enough sleep – and lead by example
  • Best Hong Kong restaurants of ex-TVB and CCTV presenter James Chau, from Casa Lisboa to the Ladies Recreation Club
  • How big is too big when it comes to K-pop groups like NCT? With three more singers possibly joining, how far can boy band go?
  • Hong Kong house gets an Asian holiday home vibe, with Hermès-inspired colours and warm wood all over
  • Kang So-yeon from Netflix show Single’s Inferno on a K-pop return, her new song Loca Loca and her 15 years chasing an entertainment career
  • Why sunscreen for black and brown-skinned people is vital, and which formulas work best
  • Taika Waititi, Thor: Love and Thunder director also known for Jojo Rabbit and Hunt for the Wilderpeople, is a Hollywood powerhouse
  • Why is Nick Kyrgios called the bad boy of tennis? Wimbledon’s ‘Wild Thing’ has racked up US$800,000 in fines for bad behaviour ... and was accused of assaulting his ex-girlfriend in Australia
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Chanel’s autumn/winter 2022-23 collection by Virginie Viard is a dreamy vision of gentle colours, lines and shapes – and statement details
  • Giambattista Valli’s 10th anniversary in haute couture at Paris Fashion Week: the autumn/winter 2022-23 collection was all colourful tulle dresses and shimmering body suits
  • Who is Gautam Adani’s wife, Priti? The spouse of Asia’s new richest man fronts the charitable Adani Foundation, is a qualified dentist, and loves gardening and reading about start-ups
  • Meet Prince Christian of Denmark, son of Crown Prince Frederik and Princess Mary – his parents just pulled the 16-year-old out of Herlufsholm School due to shocking revelations
  • Who is the richest Kardashian-Jenner beau? Net worths, ranked – from Kim’s BF Pete Davidson’s SNL earnings, to Kylie’s baby daddy Travis Scott and Kourtney’s husband Travis Barker’s music millions
  • How did LeBron James become the first active NBA billionaire? The Lakers and Space Jam star made it with a little help from Adele’s boyfriend Rich Paul … and a lifetime deal with Nike
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
